records.ety: [OE. enflamen, OF. enflamer, F. enflammer, L. inflammare, inflammatum; pref. in- in + flammare to flame, fr. flamma flame. See Flame.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Inflamed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Inflaming.]

defs: To set on fire; to kindle; to cause to burn, flame, or glow.
quotes: We should have made retreat By light of the inflamed fleet.

defs: Fig.: To kindle or intensify, as passion or appetite; to excite to an excessive or unnatural action or heat; as, to inflame desire.
quotes: Though more, it seems, Inflamed with lust than rage.
But, O inflame and fire our hearts.

defs: To provoke to anger or rage; to exasperate; to irritate; to incense; to enrage.
quotes: It will inflame you; it will make you mad.

defs: To put in a state of inflammation; to produce morbid heat, congestion, or swelling, of; as, to inflame the eyes by overwork.

quotes: A friend exaggerates a man's virtues, an enemy inflames his crimes.

defs: To grow morbidly hot, congested, or painful; to become angry or incensed.
